content_en:
They bowed before the King and admitted that there was such a

“A Prophet (3), named Ahmed, will come after me. Accept him and pledge allegiance to him for he will be a True Prophet.”

When he heard this statement from the padres King Najashi spontaneously said that if he had not had the heavy responsibilities of government he would have immediately presented himself at the feet of the Last True Prophet 38 and travelled to Makkah.

Then addressing the Muslims he said that they could stay wherever they wished in his country, and instructed that from then onwards they were to be treated as state guests. He returned the gifts to ‘Amr bin AL’As and said that he could not accept bribes as Allah Almighty had not given him his country in return for a bribe. He said that at one stage he had lost the country, but Allah 3% had in His Mercy returned it to him. He could never forget the time when the people had rebelled against his father and killed him; his uncle had been made king. He had been taken out of the royal palace and sold to an Arab trader. One night lightening struck his uncle and he died; none of his twelve sons was capable of ruling the kingdom. Out of sheer necessity the people had searched for the trader; paid him the price, which he demanded and brought me back and enthroned. This kingdom was a blessing from Allah Almighty. King Najashi was singing Praises of Allah Almighty as he sat on his throne, and ‘Amr bin Al-’As, and his companion, ‘Abdullah bin Abi Rabee’ah who were listening to him were very embarrassed. But this satiation was hopeful was very encouraging for Ja’far bin Abi Talib % and his friends. The intelligent and rational speech given by Ja’far bin Abi Talib was as follows:

“Your Royal Majesty, we were idol worshippers, and cruelty and_ harshness were part of our existence. Every evil was found in its highest degree in us. We did not value a good name or care for respectability. Having Mercy on our barbaric condition Allah 3% in His Infinite Mercy sent a Messenger to us. In a short time this True Prophet 38 brought about a radical change in us.

His teachings brought purity into our lives and changed our characters completely. He taught us about the Pure Oneness of Allah Almighty, and our thoughts turned towards worship and

prayer, He also changed the direction and goals of our hearts and taught us to give charity to the poor. He taught us to tell the truth, behave well with our kith and kin and avoid lewdness and vulgarity. He told us it was wrong to swallow what belonged to the orphan and to avoid backbiting and defaming the characters of pure women. He taught us to be good to our neighbours. When we obeyed him and put his teachings into practice, we found a revolutionary change in our lives. We who were highway robbers became guides helping and steering others to the right path. Our hearts no longer longed for sins, but yearned to do good deeds. Our hearts were so moved and softened that we learned to cry.”

When the Quraish and their allies saw this change in us they just could not tolerate it. They started to put obstacles in our way. They started persecuting us and torturing us. We could not put up with their tyranny and despotism and finally left our beloved hometown, Makkah. Having suffered all kinds of troubles and tired of being beaten up, we came to your country hoping that you would not be cruel or unjust. Relying on your well-known benevolence and kindness, we hope to be given the opportunity to live here in peace.

It seemed as if King Najashi was transported into an ecstasy, when he heard the speech of Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4%. Very gently and affectionately he asked him to kindly recite to him some of the Ayaht revealed to the Prophet 3%. Ja’far bin Abi Talib <4 recited part of Sarah Maryam, and the King was very pleased and said that it seemed that the origin of these Verses and of those revealed to Isa ps seemed to be the same.

When the present bishop in the court heard the Ayét of seerah Maryam they were moved to tears. King Najashi wanted to hear more of the Noble Quran, saying it was beautiful and was truly the Word of Allah.

Thinking it was an opportune moment, Ja’far bin Abi Talib 4% asked the King toask’Amr bin Al-‘As and his companions what their crime was. Had they murdered anyone? Did they owe money to anyone? Were they slaves who had run away? Had they infringed upon the rights of any person? Had they stolen anybody’s property? Why had the Quraish sent these people after them?

King Najashi asked ‘Amr bin Al-‘As and his companion, ‘Abdullah bin Abu Rabee‘ah, what crime had been committed by these people? When they saw the regal majesty and wrath of Najashi they became nervous, and sent away from his court, having failed in their mission. Booth left the King’s court dragging their feet, abject and ashamed. They reached Makkah and_ narrated in detail what had happened in the court in Ethiopia. The Quraish were even more inflamed and immediately boycotted the Muslims.

Finally they planned that they would murder the Prophet 3&, but they failed in their attempt. Allah Almighty took away his Prophet 3% from their midst and provided the means by which he could reach AlMadinah, safe and sound. The Quraish pursued the Muslims even there to annihilate them completely, and asa result the two wars of Badr and Uhad were waged.

In the Battle of the Trenches the enemy made elaborate preparations to make a sudden assault on the Muslims. ‘Amr bin Al-’As took part in all these battles, but his heart was overawed and impressed by Islam. Then one day the idea came to him that acceptance of Islam would bring deliverance; he set out from Makkah to pledge his faith to the One True Allah and His Messenger. On the way he saw Khalid bin Waleed and ‘Uthman bin Talhah, who were on their way to Al-Madinah.

When Khalid bin Waleed saw him he asked him where he was planning to go. ‘Amr bin AL’As asked him where he was going. Khalid replied he was going to meet the Prophet 4 and swear allegiance to Islam. ‘Amr bin Al-’As replied:

“T have the same desire. Since our destination and objective is the same, and we had lost our way in the same manner, why don’t we travel together? Talking on topics of common interest on our journey we reached Al-Madinah in the month of Safar in 8 Hijrah. We went in the presence of the Prophet 38 and swore allegiance to Islam, in his presence. He was very benevolent and kind and said that Makkah had thrown its favourite sons towards them. First Khalid bin Waleed had the honour to swear the oath of allegiance and then ‘Uthman bin Talhah and finally | received this honour.”

Since some time before accepting Islam, in his mind, ‘Amr bin AlAs & had begun to feel a liking and inclination this pure religion. But the authority and influence of his father, and the arrogance and pride of the Quraish, had stood in his way from openly declaring himself a Muslim. He could feel very strongly and intensely that the Muslims were becoming stronger day by day. Islam was rising very rapidly and the Muslim was becoming a force to reckon with. Whoever opposed them faced a crushing defeat; it was as if they are fated to fail. At every moment he felt that Islam was spreading over the whole region rapidly and people in large numbers were eagerly entering its fold. He felt that Islam would finally triumph and every other power would be crushed and reduced to smithereens.

All the rebellious and malicious plots of his father and his friends and kindred could not harm the Prophet 3% and his fellow Muslims in any way. These thoughts and feelings became so deeply embedded in his heart and mind that he was constantly engrossed in thinking of what to do next. Restlessness and anxiety seemed to perturb and overpower him.

Things came to such a pass that he gathered together those companions of his who relied and depended on him, and accepted his word as_ the last word in all matters. They trusted him absolutely and felt it was an honour to walk on the path chosen by him. He told them:

“1 feel that the Muslim will get ascendancy and reach it zenith. Islam is like a magnet, attracting and enamouring people very fast. Anyone who opposes it is bound to face defeat; at this critical juncture it would be better for us to migrate to Ethiopia. The king there, Najashi, is a very kind and soft-hearted man and we can find refuge there. It would be better to live as subjects of Najashi, than live subjugated by the Muslim. There would be an added advantage; if the Muslims take over our land we will escape from their cruelty and tyranny. On the other hand, if our people overcome them, there is nothing to prevent us from returning to own country.

He continues to say:

“All_ my companions said that you are true and agreed with my plan, and were willing to migrate to Ethiopia. Then I suggested

that we carry some expensive gifts with us to present them to the King. This would help to soften his heart towards us. We bought some very fine quality leather and some other things and set out for Ethiopia. When we reached the court of the King we entered his presence bowing our heads very respectfully, and offered our gifts to him. As we were entering, we saw ‘Amr bin Umayyah Zamri leaving the court. The Prophet 38 had sent him as an ambassador.”

After the King had accepted our gifts with pleasure, I took what! considered an opportune moment to request him to hand over the man, who had just left his presence, as I wished to kill him. The moment I uttered this phrase, King Najashi got into a towering royal rage and asked wrathfully if I wanted to kill the ambassador of the Prophet 3. He was the being to whom Allah Almighty sent revelations through His Angel, the every same Angel who had visited Masa >X\ a and other Prophets of Allah. He told me | should fear the Wrath of Allah Almighty. When I saw His Majesty’s grandeur and wrath, I began to tremble all over. When his anger subsided somewhat he spoke to me in very gentle tones.
content_id:
Mereka bersujud di hadapan Raja dan mengakui bahwa memang ada pernyataan seperti itu

"Seorang Nabi ﷺ, yang bernama Ahmad, akan datang setelahku. Terimalah dia dan berbaiat kepadanya karena dia adalah seorang Nabi yang benar."

Ketika mendengar pernyataan ini dari para pendeta, Raja Najashi secara spontan berkata bahwa seandainya dia tidak memikul tanggung jawab berat pemerintahan, dia akan segera menghadap ke hadapan Nabi Terakhir yang benar ﷺ dan melakukan perjalanan ke Makkah.

Kemudian menyapa kaum Muslimin, dia berkata bahwa mereka boleh tinggal di mana saja yang mereka inginkan di negaranya, dan memerintahkan agar mulai saat itu mereka diperlakukan sebagai tamu negara. Dia mengembalikan hadiah-hadiah itu kepada 'Amr bin Al-'As dan berkata bahwa dia tidak bisa menerima suap karena Allah ﷻ tidak memberikan negaranya dengan imbalan suap. Dia berkata bahwa pada suatu masa dia pernah kehilangan negaranya, tetapi Allah ﷻ dengan rahmat-Nya mengembalikannya. Dia tidak pernah bisa melupakan masa ketika rakyat memberontak terhadap ayahnya dan membunuhnya; pamannya dijadikan raja. Dia dikeluarkan dari istana kerajaan dan dijual kepada seorang pedagang Arab. Suatu malam petir menyambar pamannya dan dia meninggal; tidak seorang pun dari kedua belas putranya mampu memerintah kerajaan. Karena terpaksa, rakyat mencari pedagang itu; membayar harga yang dimintanya dan membawaku kembali serta menobatkanku. Kerajaan ini adalah karunia dari Allah ﷻ. Raja Najashi memuji-muji Allah ﷻ saat duduk di singgasananya, dan 'Amr bin Al-'As beserta rekannya, 'Abdullah bin Abi Rabee'ah, yang mendengarkannya merasa sangat malu. Tetapi situasi ini sangat menggembirakan bagi Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه dan teman-temannya. Pidato yang cerdas dan rasional yang disampaikan oleh Ja'far bin Abi Talib adalah sebagai berikut:

"Yang Mulia Raja, kami dahulu adalah penyembah berhala, dan kekejaman serta kekerasan merupakan bagian dari keberadaan kami. Segala kejahatan ditemukan dalam derajat tertinggi pada diri kami. Kami tidak menghargai nama baik atau peduli akan kehormatan. Mengasihani kondisi barbar kami, Allah ﷻ dengan Rahmat-Nya yang Tak Terbatas mengutus seorang Rasul kepada kami. Dalam waktu singkat Nabi yang benar ini ﷺ membawa perubahan radikal pada diri kami.

Ajaran-ajarannya membawa kesucian ke dalam kehidupan kami dan mengubah karakter kami sepenuhnya. Beliau mengajarkan kami tentang Keesaan Murni Allah ﷻ, dan pikiran kami beralih kepada ibadah dan

salah. Beliau juga mengubah arah dan tujuan hati kami serta mengajarkan kami untuk memberikan sedekah kepada orang miskin. Beliau mengajarkan kami untuk berkata jujur, bersikap baik kepada kerabat dan saudara serta menghindari kekejian dan keburukan. Beliau menyampaikan bahwa memakan harta anak yatim adalah salah dan agar menghindari ghibah serta mencemarkan kehormatan wanita suci. Beliau mengajarkan kami untuk berbuat baik kepada tetangga kami. Ketika kami menaatinya dan mengamalkan ajarannya, kami menemukan perubahan revolusioner dalam kehidupan kami. Kami yang tadinya perampok jalanan menjadi pembimbing yang membantu dan mengarahkan orang lain ke jalan yang benar. Hati kami tidak lagi merindukan dosa, tetapi mendambakan amal saleh. Hati kami begitu tersentuh dan dilunakkan sehingga kami belajar menangis."

Ketika kaum Quraisy dan sekutu-sekutu mereka melihat perubahan ini pada diri kami, mereka sama sekali tidak bisa mentoleransinya. Mereka mulai merintangi jalan kami. Mereka mulai menganiaya dan menyiksa kami. Kami tidak sanggup lagi menanggung kezaliman dan despotisme mereka dan akhirnya meninggalkan kota tercinta kami, Makkah. Setelah menderita berbagai macam kesulitan dan lelah dipukuli, kami datang ke negeri Anda dengan harapan bahwa Anda tidak akan berlaku kejam atau tidak adil. Bersandar pada kemurahan hati dan kebaikan Anda yang terkenal, kami berharap diberi kesempatan untuk hidup di sini dengan damai.

Tampak seolah-olah Raja Najashi terbawa dalam kekhusyukan ketika mendengar pidato Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه. Dengan sangat lembut dan penuh kasih sayang dia memintanya untuk membacakan beberapa ayat yang diwahyukan kepada Nabi ﷺ. Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه membacakan bagian dari Surah Maryam, dan Raja sangat senang dan berkata bahwa tampaknya asal-usul ayat-ayat ini dan ayat-ayat yang diwahyukan kepada Isa عليه السلام tampak sama.

Ketika para uskup yang hadir di istana mendengar ayat-ayat Surah Maryam, mereka tergerak hingga menangis. Raja Najashi ingin mendengar lebih banyak dari Al-Quran yang mulia, seraya berkata bahwa itu sangat indah dan benar-benar merupakan Firman Allah.

Merasa bahwa itu adalah momen yang tepat, Ja'far bin Abi Talib رضي الله عنه meminta Raja untuk bertanya kepada 'Amr bin Al-'As dan rekan-rekannya apa kejahatan mereka. Apakah mereka telah membunuh seseorang? Apakah mereka berhutang uang kepada seseorang? Apakah mereka budak yang melarikan diri? Apakah mereka telah melanggar hak seseorang? Apakah mereka telah mencuri harta seseorang? Mengapa kaum Quraisy mengirim orang-orang ini mengejar mereka?

Raja Najashi bertanya kepada 'Amr bin Al-'As dan rekannya, 'Abdullah bin Abu Rabee'ah, kejahatan apa yang telah dilakukan oleh orang-orang ini? Ketika mereka melihat keagungan dan kemurkaan raja Najashi, mereka menjadi gugup, dan diusir dari istananya, gagal dalam misi mereka. Keduanya meninggalkan istana Raja dengan menyeret kaki mereka, hina dan malu. Mereka tiba di Makkah dan menceritakan secara rinci apa yang terjadi di istana di Ethiopia. Kaum Quraisy semakin murka dan segera memboikot kaum Muslimin.

Akhirnya mereka merencanakan untuk membunuh Nabi ﷺ, tetapi mereka gagal dalam usaha mereka. Allah ﷻ membawa Nabi-Nya ﷺ dari tengah-tengah mereka dan menyediakan sarana agar beliau bisa sampai ke Al-Madinah dengan selamat. Kaum Quraisy mengejar kaum Muslimin bahkan ke sana untuk memusnahkan mereka sepenuhnya, dan akibatnya dua perang Badr dan Uhud terjadi.

Dalam Perang Khandaq, musuh membuat persiapan yang matang untuk melakukan serangan mendadak terhadap kaum Muslimin. 'Amr bin Al-'As ikut serta dalam semua pertempuran ini, tetapi hatinya terkagum dan terkesan oleh Islam. Kemudian suatu hari muncul gagasan dalam benaknya bahwa menerima Islam akan membawa keselamatan; dia berangkat dari Makkah untuk menyatakan imannya kepada Allah Yang Maha Esa dan Rasul-Nya. Di perjalanan dia bertemu Khalid bin Waleed dan 'Uthman bin Talhah, yang sedang dalam perjalanan ke Al-Madinah.

Ketika Khalid bin Waleed melihatnya, dia bertanya ke mana dia berencana pergi. 'Amr bin Al-'As balik bertanya ke mana dia akan pergi. Khalid menjawab bahwa dia akan menemui Nabi ﷺ dan bersumpah setia kepada Islam. 'Amr bin Al-'As menjawab:

"Aku pun memiliki keinginan yang sama. Karena tujuan dan sasaran kita sama, dan kita tersesat dengan cara yang sama, mengapa kita tidak bepergian bersama? Sambil berbincang tentang topik-topik yang menjadi minat bersama dalam perjalanan, kami tiba di Al-Madinah pada bulan Safar tahun 8 Hijriah. Kami menghadap Nabi ﷺ dan bersumpah setia kepada Islam di hadapan beliau. Beliau sangat murah hati dan baik serta berkata bahwa Makkah telah melemparkan putra-putra kesayangannya kepada mereka. Pertama Khalid bin Waleed yang mendapat kehormatan untuk bersumpah setia, kemudian 'Uthman bin Talhah, dan akhirnya aku mendapat kehormatan ini."

Sejak beberapa waktu sebelum menerima Islam, dalam benaknya, 'Amr bin Al-'As رضي الله عنه telah mulai merasakan ketertarikan dan kecenderungan terhadap agama yang suci ini. Tetapi otoritas dan pengaruh ayahnya, serta keangkuhan dan kesombongan kaum Quraisy, telah menghalanginya untuk secara terbuka menyatakan dirinya seorang Muslim. Dia bisa merasakan dengan sangat kuat dan mendalam bahwa kaum Muslimin semakin kuat dari hari ke hari. Islam berkembang dengan sangat pesat dan kaum Muslimin menjadi kekuatan yang harus diperhitungkan. Siapa pun yang menentang mereka menghadapi kekalahan telak; seolah-olah mereka ditakdirkan untuk gagal. Setiap saat dia merasakan bahwa Islam menyebar ke seluruh wilayah dengan cepat dan orang-orang dalam jumlah besar dengan penuh semangat memasuki ajarannya. Dia merasa bahwa Islam pada akhirnya akan menang dan setiap kekuatan lain akan dihancurkan dan diluluhlantakkan.

Semua rencana pemberontakan dan jahat dari ayahnya serta teman-teman dan kerabatnya tidak bisa merugikan Nabi ﷺ dan sesama Muslim dengan cara apa pun. Pikiran dan perasaan ini menjadi begitu tertanam dalam hati dan pikirannya sehingga dia terus-menerus terlarut dalam memikirkan apa yang harus dilakukan selanjutnya. Kegelisahan dan kecemasan tampaknya mengganggu dan menguasainya.

Keadaan sampai pada titik di mana dia mengumpulkan para sahabat yang mengandalkan dan bergantung padanya, dan menerima perkataannya sebagai kata terakhir dalam segala urusan. Mereka mempercayainya sepenuhnya dan merasa bahwa berjalan di jalan yang dipilihnya adalah suatu kehormatan. Dia berkata kepada mereka:

"Aku merasa bahwa kaum Muslimin akan memperoleh kemenangan dan mencapai puncaknya. Islam bagaikan magnet, menarik dan memikat orang dengan sangat cepat. Siapa pun yang menentangnya pasti menghadapi kekalahan; di saat kritis ini lebih baik bagi kita untuk hijrah ke Ethiopia. Raja di sana, Najashi, adalah orang yang sangat baik hati dan lembut, dan kita bisa berlindung di sana. Lebih baik hidup sebagai rakyat Najashi daripada hidup ditaklukkan oleh kaum Muslimin. Akan ada keuntungan tambahan; jika kaum Muslimin mengambil alih tanah kita, kita akan terhindar dari kekejaman dan kezaliman mereka. Di sisi lain, jika kaum kita mengalahkan mereka, tidak ada yang menghalangi kita untuk kembali ke negeri sendiri.

Ia melanjutkan perkataannya:

"Semua sahabatku berkata bahwa engkau benar dan setuju dengan rencanaku, serta bersedia untuk berhijrah ke Ethiopia. Kemudian aku mengusulkan

agar kami membawa beberapa hadiah mahal untuk dipersembahkan kepada Raja. Hal ini akan membantu melunakkan hatinya terhadap kami. Kami membeli beberapa kulit berkualitas sangat bagus dan beberapa barang lainnya, lalu berangkat menuju Ethiopia. Ketika kami tiba di istana Raja, kami memasuki kehadirannya dengan menundukkan kepala penuh hormat, dan mempersembahkan hadiah-hadiah kami kepadanya. Saat kami masuk, kami melihat 'Amr bin Umayyah Zamri sedang meninggalkan istana. Nabi ﷺ telah mengutusnya sebagai duta besar."

Setelah Raja menerima hadiah-hadiah kami dengan senang hati, aku mengambil waktu yang kuanggap tepat untuk memintanya menyerahkan orang yang baru saja meninggalkan kehadirannya, karena aku ingin membunuhnya. Begitu aku mengucapkan kalimat itu, Raja Najashi murka dengan kemarahan yang luar biasa dan bertanya dengan geram apakah aku ingin membunuh duta besar Nabi ﷺ. Beliau adalah makhluk yang kepadanya Allah Yang Mahakuasa menurunkan wahyu melalui Malaikat-Nya, Malaikat yang sama yang pernah mengunjungi Musa عليه السلام dan para Nabi Allah lainnya. Ia memberitahuku agar aku takut akan Murka Allah Yang Mahakuasa. Ketika aku melihat keagungan dan kemarahan Baginda, aku mulai gemetar seluruh tubuh. Ketika kemarahannya agak mereda, ia berbicara kepadaku dengan nada yang sangat lembut.
